source.title: The Friendship Bench Program — Interview with Dr. Chibanda

sentences.text: What a wonderful idea!
How did these amazing grandmothers become a part of your program?
Grandmothers were chosen for our program because they are deeply connected to the community.
Moreover, they have all the qualities of a counselor, such as excellent listening skills, empathy, and the ability to engage in meaningful discussions.
They are also very reliable, which is important for our efforts to support mental health.
At the beginning, we started with only fourteen volunteer grandmothers.
But today, there are thousands of grandmothers who have been trained to provide Friendship Bench therapy in Zimbabwe.
That's interesting!
What happens after patients complete their sessions with the grandmothers?
When the sessions with the grandmothers are over, patients are encouraged to join a community support group made up of former patients of the program.
The members of these groups can easily relate to and support one another.
This is because most of them come from the same community and have experienced similar struggles.
That sounds like a great way to continue the support and build a sense of community.
And the program has been incredibly successful, right?
That's correct.
In 2022, we provided face-to-face counseling to about 90,000 patients in Zimbabwe.
Now, Friendship Benches are available in more than twenty different regions around the country.
Our vision is for everyone to have a Friendship Bench within walking distance, and we are working hard to make it happen.
We've also started expanding the program, and we now offer online counseling through an app.
This has helped us reach even more people.

problems.instruction: 다음 글의 주제로 가장 적절한 것은?
problems.passage: What a wonderful idea! How did these amazing grandmothers become a part of your program? Grandmothers were chosen for our program because they are deeply connected to the community. Moreover, they have all the qualities of a counselor, such as excellent listening skills, empathy, and the ability to engage in meaningful discussions. They are also very reliable, which is important for our efforts to support mental health. At the beginning, we started with only fourteen volunteer grandmothers. But today, there are thousands of grandmothers who have been trained to provide Friendship Bench therapy in Zimbabwe. That's interesting! What happens after patients complete their sessions with the grandmothers? When the sessions with the grandmothers are over, patients are encouraged to join a community support group made up of former patients of the program. The members of these groups can easily relate to and support one another. This is because most of them come from the same community and have experienced similar struggles. That sounds like a great way to continue the support and build a sense of community. And the program has been incredibly successful, right? That's correct. In 2022, we provided face-to-face counseling to about 90,000 patients in Zimbabwe. Now, Friendship Benches are available in more than twenty different regions around the country. Our vision is for everyone to have a Friendship Bench within walking distance, and we are working hard to make it happen. We've also started expanding the program, and we now offer online counseling through an app. This has helped us reach even more people.
choices: the medical qualifications required for professional counselors in Zimbabwe
the growth and community-based approach of the Friendship Bench mental health program
the comparison between online therapy and traditional face-to-face counseling methods
the challenges of training elderly volunteers for healthcare services in rural areas
the history of community support groups in sub-Saharan African countries
problems.explanation: 할머니들이 지역사회와 깊이 연결되어 있어 상담사로 선발되었고, 14명에서 수천 명으로 성장했으며, 환자들의 커뮤니티 지원 그룹과 온라인 상담까지 확장된 Friendship Bench 프로그램의 성장과 지역사회 기반 접근이 핵심 주제이다. ②는 전문 자격증, ③은 온라인 대 대면 비교, ④는 훈련의 어려움, ⑤는 사하라 이남 아프리카 역사로 본문과 다르다.

problems.instruction: 다음 글의 요지로 가장 적절한 것은?
problems.passage: Grandmothers were chosen for our program because they are deeply connected to the community. Moreover, they have all the qualities of a counselor, such as excellent listening skills, empathy, and the ability to engage in meaningful discussions. They are also very reliable, which is important for our efforts to support mental health. At the beginning, we started with only fourteen volunteer grandmothers. But today, there are thousands of grandmothers who have been trained to provide Friendship Bench therapy in Zimbabwe. When the sessions with the grandmothers are over, patients are encouraged to join a community support group made up of former patients of the program. The members of these groups can easily relate to and support one another. In 2022, we provided face-to-face counseling to about 90,000 patients in Zimbabwe. Now, Friendship Benches are available in more than twenty different regions around the country. Our vision is for everyone to have a Friendship Bench within walking distance, and we are working hard to make it happen. We've also started expanding the program, and we now offer online counseling through an app. This has helped us reach even more people.
choices: Mental health programs should only be administered by certified professionals to ensure quality.
Online counseling apps are more efficient than face-to-face therapy for treating mental health issues.
Government funding is the most critical factor in expanding public health services in developing nations.
Elderly volunteers lack the training needed to provide meaningful psychological support to patients.
Community-based mental health support led by trusted local figures can grow into a highly effective program.
problems.explanation: 할머니들이라는 지역사회의 신뢰받는 인물이 이끄는 정신건강 지원이 14명에서 수천 명으로, 대면에서 온라인까지 확장된 성공적 프로그램이 되었다는 요지이다. ①은 전문가만 가능하다는 반대 주장, ③은 온라인이 더 효율적이라는 비교, ④는 정부 자금이 핵심이라는 언급 없는 내용, ⑤는 본문과 반대되는 주장이다.

problems.instruction: Dr. Chibanda가 할머니들을 프로그램에 선발한 이유로 가장 적절한 것은?
problems.passage: What a wonderful idea! How did these amazing grandmothers become a part of your program? Grandmothers were chosen for our program because they are deeply connected to the community. Moreover, they have all the qualities of a counselor, such as excellent listening skills, empathy, and the ability to engage in meaningful discussions. They are also very reliable, which is important for our efforts to support mental health. At the beginning, we started with only fourteen volunteer grandmothers. But today, there are thousands of grandmothers who have been trained to provide Friendship Bench therapy in Zimbabwe. That's interesting! What happens after patients complete their sessions with the grandmothers? When the sessions with the grandmothers are over, patients are encouraged to join a community support group made up of former patients of the program. The members of these groups can easily relate to and support one another. This is because most of them come from the same community and have experienced similar struggles. That sounds like a great way to continue the support and build a sense of community. And the program has been incredibly successful, right? That's correct. In 2022, we provided face-to-face counseling to about 90,000 patients in Zimbabwe. Now, Friendship Benches are available in more than twenty different regions around the country. Our vision is for everyone to have a Friendship Bench within walking distance, and we are working hard to make it happen. We've also started expanding the program, and we now offer online counseling through an app. This has helped us reach even more people.
choices: to reduce the overall cost of professional mental health services in the country
to replace the existing healthcare workers who were underperforming
because they possess counselor-like qualities and strong community ties
because they had already received formal training in psychology
to attract international funding for the Friendship Bench program
problems.explanation: ③번 문장에서 '지역사회와 깊이 연결되어 있기 때문에', ④번 문장에서 '상담사의 자질(경청, 공감, 의미 있는 대화 능력)을 갖추고 있다'가 선발 이유로 명시되어 있다. ①은 비용 절감, ③은 기존 의료진 대체, ④는 심리학 훈련 이수, ⑤는 국제 자금 유치로 본문에 없는 내용이다.

problems.instruction: 밑줄 친 부분이 의미하는 바로 가장 적절한 것은?
problems.passage: What a wonderful idea! How did these amazing grandmothers become a part of your program? Grandmothers were chosen for our program because they are deeply connected to the community. Moreover, they have all the qualities of a counselor, such as excellent listening skills, empathy, and the ability to engage in meaningful discussions. They are also very reliable, <u>which is important for our efforts to support mental health</u>. At the beginning, we started with only fourteen volunteer grandmothers. But today, there are thousands of grandmothers who have been trained to provide Friendship Bench therapy in Zimbabwe. That's interesting! What happens after patients complete their sessions with the grandmothers? When the sessions with the grandmothers are over, patients are encouraged to join a community support group made up of former patients of the program. The members of these groups can easily relate to and support one another. This is because most of them come from the same community and have experienced similar struggles. That sounds like a great way to continue the support and build a sense of community. And the program has been incredibly successful, right? That's correct. In 2022, we provided face-to-face counseling to about 90,000 patients in Zimbabwe. Now, Friendship Benches are available in more than twenty different regions around the country. Our vision is for everyone to have a Friendship Bench within walking distance, and we are working hard to make it happen. We've also started expanding the program, and we now offer online counseling through an app. This has helped us reach even more people.
choices: Mental health can only be treated through long-term hospitalization programs.
The government officially recognizes the grandmothers as licensed health professionals.
Reliability is a quality that patients develop after completing their therapy sessions.
The grandmothers' reliability makes it possible to sustain consistent mental health support.
The program depends entirely on financial donations from international organizations.
problems.explanation: '신뢰성이 정신건강 지원 노력에 중요하다'는 할머니들의 신뢰성 덕분에 일관된 정신건강 지원이 가능하다는 의미이다. ②는 장기 입원만 가능하다는 극단적 해석, ③은 공인 의료인 인정, ④는 환자가 신뢰성을 개발한다는 오해, ⑤는 국제 기부금에 전적으로 의존한다는 근거 없는 내용이다.

problems.instruction: 밑줄 친 두 문장에서 추론할 수 있는 것으로 가장 적절한 것은?
problems.passage: What a wonderful idea! How did these amazing grandmothers become a part of your program? Grandmothers were chosen for our program because they are deeply connected to the community. Moreover, they have all the qualities of a counselor, such as excellent listening skills, empathy, and the ability to engage in meaningful discussions. They are also very reliable, which is important for our efforts to support mental health. <u>At the beginning, we started with only fourteen volunteer grandmothers. But today, there are thousands of grandmothers who have been trained to provide Friendship Bench therapy in Zimbabwe.</u> That's interesting! What happens after patients complete their sessions with the grandmothers? When the sessions with the grandmothers are over, patients are encouraged to join a community support group made up of former patients of the program. The members of these groups can easily relate to and support one another. This is because most of them come from the same community and have experienced similar struggles. That sounds like a great way to continue the support and build a sense of community. And the program has been incredibly successful, right? That's correct. In 2022, we provided face-to-face counseling to about 90,000 patients in Zimbabwe. Now, Friendship Benches are available in more than twenty different regions around the country. Our vision is for everyone to have a Friendship Bench within walking distance, and we are working hard to make it happen. We've also started expanding the program, and we now offer online counseling through an app. This has helped us reach even more people.
choices: The program experienced remarkable growth in the number of trained volunteers over time.
The original fourteen grandmothers eventually quit the program due to exhaustion.
The government mandated that every community must have at least one trained grandmother.
The training process was simplified so that anyone could qualify as a volunteer.
Most of the newly trained grandmothers work exclusively in urban hospitals.
problems.explanation: 14명에서 수천 명으로 증가한 것은 프로그램이 시간이 지나면서 훈련된 자원봉사자 수에서 놀라운 성장을 이루었음을 보여준다. ①은 탈퇴, ③은 정부 의무화, ④는 훈련 간소화, ⑤는 도시 병원 전담으로 본문에서 추론할 수 없다.

problems.instruction: 밑줄 친 부분이 의미하는 바로 가장 적절한 것은?
problems.passage: What a wonderful idea! How did these amazing grandmothers become a part of your program? Grandmothers were chosen for our program because they are deeply connected to the community. Moreover, they have all the qualities of a counselor, such as excellent listening skills, empathy, and the ability to engage in meaningful discussions. They are also very reliable, which is important for our efforts to support mental health. At the beginning, we started with only fourteen volunteer grandmothers. But today, there are thousands of grandmothers who have been trained to provide Friendship Bench therapy in Zimbabwe. That's interesting! What happens after patients complete their sessions with the grandmothers? When the sessions with the grandmothers are over, patients are encouraged to join a community support group made up of former patients of the program. <u>The members of these groups can easily relate to and support one another. This is because most of them come from the same community and have experienced similar struggles.</u> That sounds like a great way to continue the support and build a sense of community. And the program has been incredibly successful, right? That's correct. In 2022, we provided face-to-face counseling to about 90,000 patients in Zimbabwe. Now, Friendship Benches are available in more than twenty different regions around the country. Our vision is for everyone to have a Friendship Bench within walking distance, and we are working hard to make it happen. We've also started expanding the program, and we now offer online counseling through an app. This has helped us reach even more people.
choices: Former patients provide financial assistance to new members of the support group.
The support groups are led by professional therapists who guide the discussions.
Shared community backgrounds and similar hardships create a natural bond of mutual understanding.
Members are required to attend weekly meetings to maintain their membership in the group.
The groups are open only to patients who have fully recovered from their conditions.
problems.explanation: '같은 지역사회 출신이고 비슷한 어려움을 겪었기 때문에 쉽게 공감하고 서로를 지지할 수 있다'는 것은 공유된 배경과 유사한 고난이 상호 이해의 자연스러운 유대를 만든다는 의미이다. ①은 재정 지원, ③은 전문 치료사 주도, ④는 주간 참석 의무, ⑤는 완전 회복 환자만 가능이라는 근거 없는 내용이다.

problems.instruction: 밑줄 친 문장이 의미하는 바로 가장 적절한 것은?
problems.passage: What a wonderful idea! How did these amazing grandmothers become a part of your program? Grandmothers were chosen for our program because they are deeply connected to the community. Moreover, they have all the qualities of a counselor, such as excellent listening skills, empathy, and the ability to engage in meaningful discussions. They are also very reliable, which is important for our efforts to support mental health. At the beginning, we started with only fourteen volunteer grandmothers. But today, there are thousands of grandmothers who have been trained to provide Friendship Bench therapy in Zimbabwe. That's interesting! What happens after patients complete their sessions with the grandmothers? When the sessions with the grandmothers are over, patients are encouraged to join a community support group made up of former patients of the program. The members of these groups can easily relate to and support one another. This is because most of them come from the same community and have experienced similar struggles. That sounds like a great way to continue the support and build a sense of community. And the program has been incredibly successful, right? That's correct. In 2022, we provided face-to-face counseling to about 90,000 patients in Zimbabwe. Now, Friendship Benches are available in more than twenty different regions around the country. <u>Our vision is for everyone to have a Friendship Bench within walking distance, and we are working hard to make it happen.</u> We've also started expanding the program, and we now offer online counseling through an app. This has helped us reach even more people.
choices: Walking is recommended as a therapeutic activity for patients in the program.
The program plans to replace all existing hospitals with outdoor therapy benches.
Dr. Chibanda believes that exercise is the most effective form of mental health treatment.
The vision has already been fully achieved in every region of Zimbabwe.
The program aims to make mental health support physically accessible to all communities.
problems.explanation: '모든 사람이 걸어서 갈 수 있는 거리에 Friendship Bench를 갖는 것이 비전'이라는 것은 정신건강 지원을 모든 지역사회에 물리적으로 접근 가능하게 만들겠다는 목표이다. ②는 걷기를 치료 활동으로 오해, ③은 병원 대체, ④는 운동이 최선의 치료, ⑤는 이미 완전 달성이라는 오해이다.

problems.instruction: 밑줄 친 부분으로부터 추론할 수 있는 것으로 가장 적절한 것은?
problems.passage: What a wonderful idea! How did these amazing grandmothers become a part of your program? Grandmothers were chosen for our program because they are deeply connected to the community. Moreover, they have all the qualities of a counselor, such as excellent listening skills, empathy, and the ability to engage in meaningful discussions. They are also very reliable, which is important for our efforts to support mental health. At the beginning, we started with only fourteen volunteer grandmothers. But today, there are thousands of grandmothers who have been trained to provide Friendship Bench therapy in Zimbabwe. That's interesting! What happens after patients complete their sessions with the grandmothers? When the sessions with the grandmothers are over, patients are encouraged to join a community support group made up of former patients of the program. The members of these groups can easily relate to and support one another. This is because most of them come from the same community and have experienced similar struggles. That sounds like a great way to continue the support and build a sense of community. And the program has been incredibly successful, right? That's correct. In 2022, we provided face-to-face counseling to about 90,000 patients in Zimbabwe. Now, Friendship Benches are available in more than twenty different regions around the country. Our vision is for everyone to have a Friendship Bench within walking distance, and we are working hard to make it happen. <u>We've also started expanding the program, and we now offer online counseling through an app. This has helped us reach even more people.</u>
choices: The app completely replaced the original face-to-face Friendship Bench sessions.
Online counseling allowed the program to extend its support beyond geographical limitations.
The app was developed by international tech companies without local input.
Patients prefer online counseling over meeting with the grandmothers in person.
The app is available only in English and therefore excludes most local residents.
problems.explanation: 온라인 상담 앱을 통해 '더 많은 사람들에게 다가갈 수 있었다'는 것은 지리적 한계를 넘어 지원을 확장할 수 있었다는 의미로 추론된다. ①은 완전 대체, ③은 외부 개발, ④는 환자 선호도, ⑤는 영어만 가능이라는 근거 없는 내용이다.

problems.instruction: 다음 빈칸에 들어갈 말로 가장 적절한 것은?
problems.passage: What a wonderful idea! How did these amazing grandmothers become a part of your program? Grandmothers were chosen for our program because they are deeply ________________ to the community. Moreover, they have all the qualities of a counselor, such as excellent listening skills, empathy, and the ability to engage in meaningful discussions. They are also very reliable, which is important for our efforts to support mental health. At the beginning, we started with only fourteen volunteer grandmothers. But today, there are thousands of grandmothers who have been trained to provide Friendship Bench therapy in Zimbabwe. That's interesting! What happens after patients complete their sessions with the grandmothers? When the sessions with the grandmothers are over, patients are encouraged to join a community support group made up of former patients of the program. The members of these groups can easily relate to and support one another. This is because most of them come from the same community and have experienced similar struggles. That sounds like a great way to continue the support and build a sense of community. And the program has been incredibly successful, right? That's correct. In 2022, we provided face-to-face counseling to about 90,000 patients in Zimbabwe. Now, Friendship Benches are available in more than twenty different regions around the country. Our vision is for everyone to have a Friendship Bench within walking distance, and we are working hard to make it happen. We've also started expanding the program, and we now offer online counseling through an app. This has helped us reach even more people.
choices: opposed
indifferent
connected
unrelated
attracted
problems.explanation: be connected to = '~와 연결되어 있다'. 할머니들이 지역사회와 깊이 연결되어 있다는 의미. opposed(반대하는), indifferent(무관심한), unrelated(관련 없는)는 반대 의미이고, attracted(끌리는)는 문맥에 맞지 않는다.

problems.instruction: 다음 빈칸에 들어갈 말로 가장 적절한 것은?
problems.passage: What a wonderful idea! How did these amazing grandmothers become a part of your program? Grandmothers were chosen for our program because they are deeply connected to the community. Moreover, they have all the qualities of a counselor, such as excellent listening skills, empathy, and the ability to engage in ______________________. They are also very reliable, which is important for our efforts to support mental health. At the beginning, we started with only fourteen volunteer grandmothers. But today, there are thousands of grandmothers who have been trained to provide Friendship Bench therapy in Zimbabwe. That's interesting! What happens after patients complete their sessions with the grandmothers? When the sessions with the grandmothers are over, patients are encouraged to join a community support group made up of former patients of the program. The members of these groups can easily relate to and support one another. This is because most of them come from the same community and have experienced similar struggles. That sounds like a great way to continue the support and build a sense of community. And the program has been incredibly successful, right? That's correct. In 2022, we provided face-to-face counseling to about 90,000 patients in Zimbabwe. Now, Friendship Benches are available in more than twenty different regions around the country. Our vision is for everyone to have a Friendship Bench within walking distance, and we are working hard to make it happen. We've also started expanding the program, and we now offer online counseling through an app. This has helped us reach even more people.
choices: meaningful discussions
competitive debates
meaningless chatter
financial planning
medical procedures
problems.explanation: 상담사의 자질로 경청, 공감과 함께 '의미 있는 대화에 참여하는 능력'이 열거되었다. competitive debates(경쟁적 토론), meaningless chatter(무의미한 수다), financial planning(재무 설계), medical procedures(의료 시술)는 상담사의 자질과 어울리지 않는다.

problems.instruction: 다음 빈칸에 들어갈 말로 가장 적절한 것은?
problems.passage: What a wonderful idea! How did these amazing grandmothers become a part of your program? Grandmothers were chosen for our program because they are deeply connected to the community. Moreover, they have all the qualities of a counselor, such as excellent listening skills, empathy, and the ability to engage in meaningful discussions. They are also very ________________, which is important for our efforts to support mental health. At the beginning, we started with only fourteen volunteer grandmothers. But today, there are thousands of grandmothers who have been trained to provide Friendship Bench therapy in Zimbabwe. That's interesting! What happens after patients complete their sessions with the grandmothers? When the sessions with the grandmothers are over, patients are encouraged to join a community support group made up of former patients of the program. The members of these groups can easily relate to and support one another. This is because most of them come from the same community and have experienced similar struggles. That sounds like a great way to continue the support and build a sense of community. And the program has been incredibly successful, right? That's correct. In 2022, we provided face-to-face counseling to about 90,000 patients in Zimbabwe. Now, Friendship Benches are available in more than twenty different regions around the country. Our vision is for everyone to have a Friendship Bench within walking distance, and we are working hard to make it happen. We've also started expanding the program, and we now offer online counseling through an app. This has helped us reach even more people.
choices: unpredictable
competitive
reliable
independent
cautious
problems.explanation: 정신건강 지원 노력에 중요한 자질로 '신뢰할 수 있는(reliable)'이 적절하다. unpredictable(예측 불가능한)은 반대, competitive(경쟁적인), independent(독립적인), cautious(조심스러운)는 문맥상 부적절하다.

problems.instruction: 다음 빈칸에 들어갈 말로 가장 적절한 것은?

Our vision is for everyone to have a Friendship Bench within walking distance, and we are working hard to make it happen. We've also started expanding the program, and we now offer online counseling through an app. This has helped us reach even more people.
problems.blankTarget: made up of former patients of the program
choices: supervised by licensed psychiatrists from the government
designed exclusively for patients under the age of thirty
funded entirely by international health organizations
made up of former patients of the program
operating only in the capital city of Zimbabwe
problems.explanation: 원문에서 지역사회 지원 그룹은 '프로그램의 이전 환자들로 구성된(made up of former patients)' 것이다. ①은 정신과 의사 감독, ③은 30세 미만 전용, ④는 국제 기구 자금, ⑤는 수도에서만 운영이라는 근거 없는 내용이다.

problems.instruction: 다음 빈칸에 들어갈 말로 가장 적절한 것은?
problems.passage: What a wonderful idea! How did these amazing grandmothers become a part of your program? Grandmothers were chosen for our program because they are deeply connected to the community. Moreover, they have all the qualities of a counselor, such as excellent listening skills, empathy, and the ability to engage in meaningful discussions. They are also very reliable, which is important for our efforts to support mental health. At the beginning, we started with only fourteen volunteer grandmothers. But today, there are thousands of grandmothers who have been trained to provide Friendship Bench therapy in Zimbabwe. That's interesting! What happens after patients complete their sessions with the grandmothers? When the sessions with the grandmothers are over, patients are encouraged to join a community support group made up of former patients of the program. The members of these groups can easily relate to and support one another. This is because ________________________________________________________________________________. That sounds like a great way to continue the support and build a sense of community. And the program has been incredibly successful, right? That's correct. In 2022, we provided face-to-face counseling to about 90,000 patients in Zimbabwe. Now, Friendship Benches are available in more than twenty different regions around the country. Our vision is for everyone to have a Friendship Bench within walking distance, and we are working hard to make it happen. We've also started expanding the program, and we now offer online counseling through an app. This has helped us reach even more people.
problems.blankTarget: most of them come from the same community and have experienced similar struggles
choices: they are all required to follow a strict program schedule together
the grandmothers personally select members who share similar interests
the government assigns patients to groups based on their medical records
they undergo the same professional training before joining the group
most of them come from the same community and have experienced similar struggles
problems.explanation: 원문 ⑫번 문장에서 '같은 지역사회 출신이고 비슷한 어려움을 겪었기 때문'이라고 명시되어 있다. ①은 엄격한 일정, ②는 할머니 선발, ④는 정부 배정, ⑤는 전문 훈련으로 본문과 다르다.

problems.instruction: 다음 빈칸에 들어갈 말로 가장 적절한 것은?
problems.passage: What a wonderful idea! How did these amazing grandmothers become a part of your program? Grandmothers were chosen for our program because they are deeply connected to the community. Moreover, they have all the qualities of a counselor, such as excellent listening skills, empathy, and the ability to engage in meaningful discussions. They are also very reliable, which is important for our efforts to support mental health. At the beginning, we started with only fourteen volunteer grandmothers. But today, there are thousands of grandmothers who have been trained to provide Friendship Bench therapy in Zimbabwe. That's interesting! What happens after patients complete their sessions with the grandmothers? When the sessions with the grandmothers are over, patients are encouraged to join a community support group made up of former patients of the program. The members of these groups can easily relate to and support one another. This is because most of them come from the same community and have experienced similar struggles. That sounds like a great way to continue the support and build a sense of community. And the program has been incredibly successful, right? That's correct. In 2022, we provided face-to-face counseling to about 90,000 patients in Zimbabwe. Now, Friendship Benches are available in more than twenty different regions around the country. Our vision is for everyone to have a Friendship Bench _______________________, and we are working hard to make it happen. We've also started expanding the program, and we now offer online counseling through an app. This has helped us reach even more people.
choices: in every hospital ward
at the nearest airport
inside government offices
within walking distance
on university campuses only
problems.explanation: 원문에서 '걸어서 갈 수 있는 거리 내에(within walking distance)' Friendship Bench가 있도록 하는 것이 비전이라고 했다. 나머지는 모두 원문과 다른 장소를 제시한다.

problems.instruction: 글의 흐름으로 보아, 주어진 문장이 들어가기에 가장 적절한 곳은?
problems.givenSentence: They are also very reliable, which is important for our efforts to support mental health.
problems.passage: Grandmothers were chosen for our program because they are deeply connected to the community. ( ① ) Moreover, they have all the qualities of a counselor, such as excellent listening skills, empathy, and the ability to engage in meaningful discussions. ( ② ) At the beginning, we started with only fourteen volunteer grandmothers. ( ③ ) But today, there are thousands of grandmothers who have been trained to provide Friendship Bench therapy in Zimbabwe. ( ④ )
problems.explanation: 주어진 문장은 할머니들의 자질에 대한 추가 설명('또한 매우 신뢰할 수 있다')이므로, 상담사 자질을 열거한 ④번 문장 뒤(②)에 들어가야 한다. 이후 '처음에는 14명으로 시작'이라는 프로그램 규모 이야기로 자연스럽게 전환된다.

problems.instruction: 글의 흐름으로 보아, 주어진 문장이 들어가기에 가장 적절한 곳은?
problems.givenSentence: This is because most of them come from the same community and have experienced similar struggles.
problems.passage: When the sessions with the grandmothers are over, patients are encouraged to join a community support group made up of former patients of the program. ( ① ) The members of these groups can easily relate to and support one another. ( ② ) That sounds like a great way to continue the support and build a sense of community. ( ③ ) And the program has been incredibly successful, right? ( ④ )
problems.explanation: 'This is because'가 앞 문장의 이유를 설명하므로, '서로 쉽게 공감하고 지지할 수 있다'는 ⑪번 문장 바로 뒤(②)에 들어가야 한다.

problems.instruction: 글의 흐름으로 보아, 주어진 문장이 들어가기에 가장 적절한 곳은?
problems.givenSentence: We've also started expanding the program, and we now offer online counseling through an app.
problems.passage: That's correct. In 2022, we provided face-to-face counseling to about 90,000 patients in Zimbabwe. ( ① ) Now, Friendship Benches are available in more than twenty different regions around the country. ( ② ) Our vision is for everyone to have a Friendship Bench within walking distance, and we are working hard to make it happen. ( ③ ) This has helped us reach even more people. ( ④ )
problems.explanation: 주어진 문장은 프로그램 확장과 온라인 상담 앱 도입을 설명하고, 바로 뒤의 'This has helped us reach even more people'에서 'This'가 온라인 상담을 가리키므로 ③이 적절하다.

problems.instruction: 주어진 글 다음에 이어질 글의 순서로 가장 적절한 것은?
problems.givenStart: How did these amazing grandmothers become a part of your program?

chunks.A: Moreover, they have all the qualities of a counselor, such as excellent listening skills, empathy, and the ability to engage in meaningful discussions. They are also very reliable, which is important for our efforts to support mental health.
chunks.B: At the beginning, we started with only fourteen volunteer grandmothers. But today, there are thousands of grandmothers who have been trained to provide Friendship Bench therapy in Zimbabwe.
chunks.C: Grandmothers were chosen for our program because they are deeply connected to the community.

problems.explanation: (C) 할머니 선발 이유(지역사회 연결) → (A) 'Moreover'로 추가 자질(경청, 공감, 신뢰성) 열거 → (B) 14명에서 수천 명으로의 프로그램 성장. 'Moreover'가 (C)의 추가 설명을 이끌므로 (C)-(A) 순서가 필수.

problems.instruction: 주어진 글 다음에 이어질 글의 순서로 가장 적절한 것은?
problems.givenStart: What happens after patients complete their sessions with the grandmothers?

chunks.A: The members of these groups can easily relate to and support one another.
chunks.B: This is because most of them come from the same community and have experienced similar struggles.
chunks.C: When the sessions with the grandmothers are over, patients are encouraged to join a community support group made up of former patients of the program.

problems.explanation: (C) 세션 후 지원 그룹 가입 권유 → (A) 그룹 구성원들이 쉽게 공감·지지 → (B) 'This is because'로 그 이유 설명. 'This is because'가 (A)의 이유를 설명하므로 (A)-(B) 순서가 필수.

problems.instruction: 다음 글의 내용과 일치하는 것은?
problems.passage: Grandmothers were chosen for our program because they are deeply connected to the community. Moreover, they have all the qualities of a counselor, such as excellent listening skills, empathy, and the ability to engage in meaningful discussions. They are also very reliable, which is important for our efforts to support mental health. At the beginning, we started with only fourteen volunteer grandmothers. But today, there are thousands of grandmothers who have been trained to provide Friendship Bench therapy in Zimbabwe. When the sessions with the grandmothers are over, patients are encouraged to join a community support group made up of former patients of the program. The members of these groups can easily relate to and support one another. This is because most of them come from the same community and have experienced similar struggles. In 2022, we provided face-to-face counseling to about 90,000 patients in Zimbabwe. Now, Friendship Benches are available in more than twenty different regions around the country.
choices: The program began with fourteen volunteer grandmothers and has since grown to thousands.
The grandmothers were selected primarily because of their medical degrees.
Patients are required to continue therapy with the grandmothers indefinitely.
The community support groups are composed of professional social workers.
Friendship Benches currently operate in only two regions of Zimbabwe.
problems.explanation: ⑥번 문장 'we started with only fourteen volunteer grandmothers'과 ⑦번 문장 'there are thousands of grandmothers'에서 확인된다. ②는 의학 학위가 아닌 지역사회 연결과 상담 자질, ③은 무기한 치료가 아닌 세션 후 지원 그룹, ④는 전문 사회복지사가 아닌 이전 환자, ⑤는 20개 이상 지역이다.

problems.instruction: 다음 글의 내용과 일치하는 것은?
problems.passage: When the sessions with the grandmothers are over, patients are encouraged to join a community support group made up of former patients of the program. The members of these groups can easily relate to and support one another. This is because most of them come from the same community and have experienced similar struggles.
choices: The support groups meet only once a year for an annual review session.
Support group members share similar backgrounds and can empathize with each other.
Patients must pay a membership fee to join the community support group.
The grandmothers lead the community support groups after the sessions end.
Only patients who have been in the program for over five years can join.
problems.explanation: ⑪~⑫번 문장에서 '같은 지역사회 출신이고 비슷한 어려움을 겪었기 때문에 쉽게 공감하고 지지한다'고 명시되어 있다. ②는 연 1회, ③은 회비, ④는 할머니 주도, ⑤는 5년 이상 참여 조건으로 본문에 없는 내용이다.

problems.instruction: 다음 글의 내용과 일치하지 않는 것은?
problems.passage: That's correct. In 2022, we provided face-to-face counseling to about 90,000 patients in Zimbabwe. Now, Friendship Benches are available in more than twenty different regions around the country. Our vision is for everyone to have a Friendship Bench within walking distance, and we are working hard to make it happen. We've also started expanding the program, and we now offer online counseling through an app. This has helped us reach even more people.
choices: About 90,000 patients received face-to-face counseling in 2022.
Friendship Benches operate in more than twenty regions in Zimbabwe.
The program's vision is for everyone to have a bench within walking distance.
An app has been developed to provide online counseling services.
The program has stopped offering face-to-face sessions in favor of online-only counseling.
problems.explanation: 본문에서 온라인 상담은 프로그램 '확장(expanding)'으로 추가된 것이지, 대면 상담을 중단한 것이 아니다. '대면 상담 대신 온라인만 제공한다'는 ④가 일치하지 않는 내용이다.

problems.instruction: 다음 글의 내용과 일치하지 않는 것은?
problems.passage: Grandmothers were chosen for our program because they are deeply connected to the community. Moreover, they have all the qualities of a counselor, such as excellent listening skills, empathy, and the ability to engage in meaningful discussions. They are also very reliable, which is important for our efforts to support mental health. At the beginning, we started with only fourteen volunteer grandmothers. But today, there are thousands of grandmothers who have been trained to provide Friendship Bench therapy in Zimbabwe.
choices: Only grandmothers with prior counseling experience were selected for the program.
The grandmothers have qualities such as listening skills and empathy.
The program initially started with fourteen volunteer grandmothers.
The grandmothers are deeply connected to their local communities.
Thousands of grandmothers have now been trained for the program.
problems.explanation: 본문에서 할머니들이 선발된 이유는 지역사회 연결, 상담사 자질, 신뢰성이며 '이전 상담 경험'이 선발 조건이라는 언급은 없다. ④가 일치하지 않는 내용이다.

problems.instruction: 다음 글의 내용과 일치하지 않는 것은?
problems.passage: Grandmothers were chosen for our program because they are deeply connected to the community. Moreover, they have all the qualities of a counselor, such as excellent listening skills, empathy, and the ability to engage in meaningful discussions. At the beginning, we started with only fourteen volunteer grandmothers. But today, there are thousands of grandmothers who have been trained to provide Friendship Bench therapy in Zimbabwe. When the sessions with the grandmothers are over, patients are encouraged to join a community support group made up of former patients of the program. In 2022, we provided face-to-face counseling to about 90,000 patients in Zimbabwe. Now, Friendship Benches are available in more than twenty different regions around the country. We've also started expanding the program, and we now offer online counseling through an app.
choices: The program has expanded to include online counseling through an app.
Grandmothers were chosen for their community connections and counselor qualities.
Patients join support groups composed of former program participants after sessions.
The program currently operates in fewer than ten regions across Zimbabwe.
The program provided face-to-face counseling to about 90,000 patients in 2022.
problems.explanation: ⑰번 문장에서 'more than twenty different regions'이라고 명시되어 있으므로, '10개 미만 지역'이라는 ⑤가 일치하지 않는다.

problems.instruction: 다음 글의 내용을 한 문장으로 요약하고자 한다. 빈칸 (A)와 (B)에 들어갈 말로 가장 적절한 것은?
problems.passage: Grandmothers were chosen for our program because they are deeply connected to the community. Moreover, they have all the qualities of a counselor, such as excellent listening skills, empathy, and the ability to engage in meaningful discussions. At the beginning, we started with only fourteen volunteer grandmothers. But today, there are thousands of grandmothers who have been trained to provide Friendship Bench therapy in Zimbabwe. When the sessions with the grandmothers are over, patients are encouraged to join a community support group made up of former patients of the program. In 2022, we provided face-to-face counseling to about 90,000 patients in Zimbabwe. We've also started expanding the program, and we now offer online counseling through an app. This has helped us reach even more people.
problems.summaryTemplate: The Friendship Bench program, which uses ____(A)____ as counselors, has grown from a small initiative into a large-scale operation that also offers ____(B)____.
choices: (A) licensed psychologists - (B) hospital-based treatment
(A) university students - (B) group therapy in schools
(A) community-connected grandmothers - (B) online counseling through an app
(A) trained social workers - (B) home-visit medical services
(A) community-connected grandmothers - (B) prescription medication services
problems.explanation: 지역사회와 연결된 할머니들(community-connected grandmothers)이 상담사 역할을 하고, 앱을 통한 온라인 상담(online counseling through an app)도 제공하게 되었다. ①은 공인 심리학자, ③은 대학생, ④는 사회복지사, ⑤는 처방약 서비스로 본문과 다르다.

problems.instruction: 다음 글의 내용을 한 문장으로 요약하고자 한다. 빈칸 (A)와 (B)에 들어갈 말로 가장 적절한 것은?
problems.passage: When the sessions with the grandmothers are over, patients are encouraged to join a community support group made up of former patients of the program. The members of these groups can easily relate to and support one another. This is because most of them come from the same community and have experienced similar struggles.
problems.summaryTemplate: After therapy sessions, patients join support groups of ____(A)____, where shared backgrounds help them ____(B)____ each other.
choices: (A) medical professionals - (B) diagnose
(A) former patients - (B) relate to and support
(A) volunteer grandmothers - (B) train
(A) government officials - (B) evaluate
(A) former patients - (B) compete with
problems.explanation: 이전 환자들(former patients)로 구성된 그룹에서 공유된 배경 덕분에 서로 공감하고 지지(relate to and support)한다. ①은 의료 전문가·진단, ③은 자원봉사 할머니·훈련, ④는 공무원·평가, ⑤는 이전 환자·경쟁으로 본문과 다르다.

problems.instruction: 밑줄 친 which가 가리키는 것으로 가장 적절한 것은?
problems.passage: What a wonderful idea! How did these amazing grandmothers become a part of your program? Grandmothers were chosen for our program because they are deeply connected to the community. Moreover, they have all the qualities of a counselor, such as excellent listening skills, empathy, and the ability to engage in meaningful discussions. They are also very reliable, <u>which</u> is important for our efforts to support mental health. At the beginning, we started with only fourteen volunteer grandmothers. But today, there are thousands of grandmothers who have been trained to provide Friendship Bench therapy in Zimbabwe. That's interesting! What happens after patients complete their sessions with the grandmothers? When the sessions with the grandmothers are over, patients are encouraged to join a community support group made up of former patients of the program. The members of these groups can easily relate to and support one another. This is because most of them come from the same community and have experienced similar struggles. That sounds like a great way to continue the support and build a sense of community. And the program has been incredibly successful, right? That's correct. In 2022, we provided face-to-face counseling to about 90,000 patients in Zimbabwe. Now, Friendship Benches are available in more than twenty different regions around the country. Our vision is for everyone to have a Friendship Bench within walking distance, and we are working hard to make it happen. We've also started expanding the program, and we now offer online counseling through an app. This has helped us reach even more people.
choices: the fact that the grandmothers are very reliable
the grandmothers' ability to engage in meaningful discussions
the community's demand for more mental health programs
the training that the grandmothers received before joining
the number of patients who completed the program
problems.explanation: 계속적 용법의 관계대명사 which는 앞 절 전체 또는 핵심 내용을 받는다. 여기서는 '할머니들이 매우 신뢰할 수 있다는 사실'을 가리킨다. ①은 대화 능력, ③은 지역사회 수요, ④는 훈련, ⑤는 환자 수로 which의 선행사와 다르다.

problems.instruction: 밑줄 친 This가 가리키는 것으로 가장 적절한 것은?
problems.passage: What a wonderful idea! How did these amazing grandmothers become a part of your program? Grandmothers were chosen for our program because they are deeply connected to the community. Moreover, they have all the qualities of a counselor, such as excellent listening skills, empathy, and the ability to engage in meaningful discussions. They are also very reliable, which is important for our efforts to support mental health. At the beginning, we started with only fourteen volunteer grandmothers. But today, there are thousands of grandmothers who have been trained to provide Friendship Bench therapy in Zimbabwe. That's interesting! What happens after patients complete their sessions with the grandmothers? When the sessions with the grandmothers are over, patients are encouraged to join a community support group made up of former patients of the program. The members of these groups can easily relate to and support one another. <u>This</u> is because most of them come from the same community and have experienced similar struggles. That sounds like a great way to continue the support and build a sense of community. And the program has been incredibly successful, right? That's correct. In 2022, we provided face-to-face counseling to about 90,000 patients in Zimbabwe. Now, Friendship Benches are available in more than twenty different regions around the country. Our vision is for everyone to have a Friendship Bench within walking distance, and we are working hard to make it happen. We've also started expanding the program, and we now offer online counseling through an app. This has helped us reach even more people.
choices: the vision of placing Friendship Benches within walking distance
the fact that thousands of grandmothers have been trained
the face-to-face counseling sessions provided in 2022
the community support groups made up of former patients
the offering of online counseling through an app
problems.explanation: This는 바로 앞 문장의 '앱을 통한 온라인 상담 제공'을 가리킨다. 온라인 상담 덕분에 더 많은 사람들에게 다가갈 수 있었다는 문맥이다. ①은 비전, ②는 훈련된 할머니, ④는 2022년 대면 상담, ⑤는 지원 그룹으로 This의 지시 대상과 다르다.

problems.instruction: 다음 글의 밑줄 친 부분 중, 어법상 틀린 것은?
problems.passage: ①<u>What a wonderful</u> idea! Grandmothers were chosen for our program because they are deeply ②<u>connected</u> to the community. Moreover, they have all the qualities of a counselor, such as excellent listening skills, empathy, and the ability ③<u>engaging</u> in meaningful discussions. They are also very reliable, ④<u>which</u> is important for our efforts to support mental health. But today, there are thousands of grandmothers ⑤<u>who have been trained</u> to provide Friendship Bench therapy.
choices: ①
②
④
⑤
③
problems.explanation: the ability to V(~할 능력)이므로 engaging → to engage. ① What a + 형용사 + 명사(감탄문) ✓ ② be connected to ✓ ④ 계속적 관계대명사 which ✓ ⑤ 관계대명사 + 현재완료수동 ✓

problems.instruction: 다음 글의 밑줄 친 부분 중, 어법상 틀린 것은?
problems.passage: Grandmothers ①<u>were chosen</u> for our program because they are deeply connected to the community. They are also very reliable, which is important for our efforts ②<u>to support</u> mental health. But today, there are thousands of grandmothers who ③<u>have been trained</u> to provide Friendship Bench therapy. When the sessions are over, patients are encouraged ④<u>joining</u> a community support group. Our vision is for everyone ⑤<u>to have</u> a Friendship Bench within walking distance.
choices: ①
②
③
⑤
④
problems.explanation: be encouraged to V(~하도록 격려받다)이므로 joining → to join. ① were chosen(수동태) ✓ ② efforts to V(~하려는 노력) ✓ ③ have been trained(현재완료수동) ✓ ⑤ for everyone to have(의미상 주어 + to부정사) ✓

problems.instruction: 다음 글의 밑줄 친 부분 중, 어법상 틀린 것은?
problems.passage: Patients are encouraged to join a community support group ①<u>made up of</u> former patients. The members of these groups can easily relate to and support ②<u>one another</u>. This is because most of them come from the same community and ③<u>have experienced</u> similar struggles. Our vision is for everyone to have a Friendship Bench within walking distance, and we are working hard to ④<u>make it happen</u>. This has helped us ⑤<u>reaching</u> even more people.
choices: ①
⑤
②
③
④
problems.explanation: help + O + V원형 / to V이므로 reaching → reach (또는 to reach). ① made up of(과거분사 후치수식) ✓ ② one another(서로) ✓ ③ have experienced(현재완료) ✓ ④ make + O + V원형(사역동사) ✓

problems.instruction: 다음 중 밑줄 친 which의 쓰임이 나머지 넷과 다른 것은?
choices: I need the book <u>which</u> is on the table.
They are very reliable, <u>which</u> is important for our efforts to support mental health.
She passed the exam, <u>which</u> surprised everyone.
He arrived late, <u>which</u> made the teacher angry.
She donated money to charity, <u>which</u> was very generous of her.
problems.explanation: ④는 제한적 용법(선행사 the book을 수식), 나머지 ①②③⑤는 모두 계속적 용법(콤마 + which, 앞 절 전체를 받음)이다.

problems.instruction: 다음 중 밑줄 친 to부정사의 용법이 나머지 넷과 다른 것은?
choices: grandmothers who have been trained <u>to provide</u> Friendship Bench therapy
patients are encouraged <u>to join</u> a community support group
we are working hard <u>to make</u> it happen
the ability <u>to engage</u> in meaningful discussions
Our vision is for everyone <u>to have</u> a Friendship Bench
problems.explanation: ①은 형용사적 용법(the ability를 수식), ②③④⑤는 모두 부사적 용법(목적)이다. ①만 명사를 수식하는 형용사적 용법으로 다르다.

problems.instruction: 다음 중 밑줄 친 that(That/That's)의 쓰임이 나머지 넷과 다른 것은?
choices: <u>That</u>'s interesting!
<u>That</u> sounds like a great way to continue the support.
I believe <u>that</u> the program has been successful.
<u>That</u>'s correct.
<u>That</u>'s really amazing!
problems.explanation: ④의 that은 접속사(명사절을 이끄는), 나머지 ①②③⑤의 That은 모두 지시대명사('그것')이다.

problems.instruction: 다음 중 밑줄 친 because의 쓰임이 나머지 넷과 다른 것은?
choices: Grandmothers were chosen <u>because</u> they are deeply connected to the community.
She studied hard <u>because</u> she wanted to pass the exam.
This is <u>because</u> most of them come from the same community.
He stayed home <u>because</u> he was feeling ill.
We left early <u>because</u> the weather was getting worse.
problems.explanation: ②의 because는 'This is because ~' 구문에서 보어절을 이끄는 접속사(주격보어)이고, 나머지 ①③④⑤는 모두 이유를 나타내는 부사절을 이끄는 접속사이다.

problems.instruction: 다음 문장에서 어법상 틀린 부분을 찾아 바르게 고치시오.
problems.stem: How a wonderful idea!
problems.explanation: What a + 형용사 + 명사! (감탄문). How + 형용사 + 주어 + 동사! 와 구별해야 한다. 명사(idea)가 있으므로 What을 써야 한다.

problems.instruction: 다음 문장에서 어법상 틀린 부분을 찾아 바르게 고치시오.
problems.stem: Grandmothers chose for our program because they are deeply connected to the community.
problems.explanation: 할머니들은 선발'된' 것이므로 수동태 were chosen이 적절하다. chose는 능동태로 의미가 달라진다.

problems.instruction: 다음 문장에서 어법상 틀린 부분을 찾아 바르게 고치시오.
problems.stem: They have all the qualities of a counselor, such as excellent listening skills, empathy, and the ability engaging in meaningful discussions.
problems.explanation: the ability to V = '~할 능력'. ability 뒤에는 to부정사가 온다.

problems.instruction: 다음 문장에서 어법상 틀린 부분을 찾아 바르게 고치시오.
problems.stem: There are thousands of grandmothers who have been training to provide Friendship Bench therapy.
problems.explanation: 할머니들이 훈련을 '받은' 것이므로 수동태 have been trained가 적절하다. have been training은 현재완료진행(능동)으로 의미가 다르다.

problems.instruction: 다음 문장에서 어법상 틀린 부분을 찾아 바르게 고치시오.
problems.stem: Patients are encouraged joining a community support group made up of former patients.
problems.explanation: be encouraged to V = '~하도록 격려받다'. encourage는 to부정사를 목적격 보어로 취한다.

problems.instruction: 다음 문장에서 어법상 틀린 부분을 찾아 바르게 고치시오.
problems.stem: Our vision is for everyone to have a Friendship Bench within walking distance, and we are working hard to make it to happen.
problems.explanation: make + O + V원형 (사역동사). make는 목적격 보어로 원형부정사를 취하므로 to가 불필요하다.

problems.instruction: 다음 문장에서 어법상 틀린 부분을 찾아 바르게 고치시오.
problems.stem: This has helped us reaching even more people.
problems.explanation: help + O + V원형 / to V. help는 동명사를 취하지 않으므로 reaching은 불가하다.

problems.instruction: 주어진 단어를 올바른 순서로 배열하여 문장을 완성하시오.
problems.answer: Grandmothers were chosen for our program because they are deeply connected
problems.explanation: Grandmothers were chosen(수동태) + for our program(전치사구) + because they are(이유절) + deeply connected to(과거분사구)

problems.instruction: 주어진 단어를 올바른 순서로 배열하여 문장을 완성하시오.
problems.stem: Moreover, they have all the qualities of a counselor, _________________________________ discussions.
problems.answer: such as excellent listening skills, empathy, and the ability to engage in meaningful
problems.explanation: such as A, B, and C(병렬 나열) + the ability to V(~할 능력) + engage in meaningful discussions(의미 있는 대화에 참여하다)

problems.instruction: 주어진 단어를 올바른 순서로 배열하여 문장을 완성하시오.
problems.stem: When the sessions are over, _________________________________ of the program.
problems.answer: patients are encouraged to join a community support group made up of former patients
problems.explanation: patients are encouraged to join(수동 + to부정사) + a community support group(목적어) + made up of former patients(과거분사 후치수식)

problems.instruction: 주어진 단어를 올바른 순서로 배열하여 문장을 완성하시오.
problems.answer: Our vision is for everyone to have a Friendship Bench within walking distance, and we are working hard
problems.explanation: Our vision is(주어+동사) + for everyone to have(의미상 주어 + to부정사) + within walking distance(부사구) + and we are working hard(접속사+부가절)

problems.instruction: 주어진 단어를 올바른 순서로 배열하여 문장을 완성하시오.
problems.answer: there are thousands of grandmothers who have been trained to provide Friendship Bench therapy
problems.explanation: there are(유도부사 구문) + thousands of grandmothers(주어) + who have been trained(관계대명사 + 현재완료수동) + to provide(to부정사 목적)

problems.instruction: 다음 빈칸에 들어갈 말의 형태로 가장 적절한 것은?
problems.stem: Grandmothers _______ (choose) for our program because they are deeply connected to the community.
choices: chose
were chosen
have choosing
are choosing
had chose
problems.explanation: 할머니들은 선발'된' 것이므로 수동태 were chosen이 적절하다.

problems.instruction: 다음 빈칸에 들어갈 말의 형태로 가장 적절한 것은?
problems.stem: They have all the qualities of a counselor, such as excellent listening skills, empathy, and the ability _______ (engage) in meaningful discussions.
choices: to engage
engage
engaging
engaged
to engaging
problems.explanation: the ability to V = '~할 능력'. ability 뒤에는 to부정사가 온다.

problems.instruction: 다음 빈칸에 들어갈 말의 형태로 가장 적절한 것은?
problems.stem: There are thousands of grandmothers who _______ (train) to provide Friendship Bench therapy in Zimbabwe.
choices: have been training
were training
have been trained
had trained
are training
problems.explanation: 할머니들이 훈련을 '받아 온' 것이므로 현재완료수동 have been trained가 적절하다. 과거에 훈련받아 현재까지 그 상태가 지속된다.

problems.instruction: 다음 빈칸에 들어갈 말의 형태로 가장 적절한 것은?
problems.stem: Patients are encouraged _______ (join) a community support group made up of former patients.
choices: join
to join
joining
joined
to joining
problems.explanation: be encouraged to V = '~하도록 격려받다'. encourage는 to부정사를 목적격 보어로 취한다.

problems.instruction: 다음 빈칸에 들어갈 말의 형태로 가장 적절한 것은?
problems.stem: This is because most of them come from the same community and _______ (experience) similar struggles.
choices: experiencing
are experienced
had experiencing
have experienced
experiences
problems.explanation: come과 병렬 구조이며 현재완료 have experienced(경험을 나타냄)가 적절하다. '비슷한 어려움을 겪어 왔다'는 경험의 현재완료.

problems.instruction: 다음 빈칸에 들어갈 말의 형태로 가장 적절한 것은?
problems.stem: We are working hard to make it _______ (happen).
choices: to happen
happening
happened
happen
to happening
problems.explanation: make + O + V원형 (사역동사). make는 목적격 보어로 원형부정사를 취한다.

problems.instruction: 다음 빈칸에 들어갈 말의 형태로 가장 적절한 것은?
problems.stem: This has helped us _______ (reach) even more people.
choices: reach
reaching
to reaching
reached
having reached
problems.explanation: help + O + V원형 / to V. reach(원형부정사)가 가장 적절하다. help는 동명사(reaching)를 취하지 않는다.

problems.instruction: 다음 빈칸에 들어갈 말의 형태로 가장 적절한 것은?
problems.stem: We've also started _______ (expand) the program, and we now offer online counseling through an app.
choices: expand
to expanding
expanded
having expanded
expanding
problems.explanation: start + V-ing / to V. '확장하기 시작했다'이므로 동명사 expanding이 적절하다. start는 동명사와 to부정사 모두 가능하지만, 선택지에 to expand가 없으므로 expanding이 정답.
